tracks.meaning: to let something go or set it free from being held or confined
to allow a person to go free, especially after being arrested or imprisoned
to make a new product, film, song, or software publicly available for the first time
a new version or update of a software application or system made available to users
to issue or publish a statement, document, or information to the public or media
to free oneself from strong emotions, tension, or psychological stress
to discharge a patient from medical care or allow them to leave a hospital
to discharge or emit a substance from the body such as hormones, fluids, or secretions
to make stored or confined material available for use or distribution
to allow something to move or flow freely after being held back or blocked
to let go of or throw a ball or projectile in a game or athletic activity
to officially distribute a song, album, or musical recording to the public
a theatrical production or performance that is made available to audiences
the act of making funds or assets available for use or withdrawal
the action of making a new version of software available for download and installation
